(2 comments)

This is OK as far as it goes (I might even have a patch locally that adds something similar).  The main limitation is that this doesn't allow creating files with complex layouts (e.g. PFL or FLR).

That probably isn't a huge limitation immediately, but it probably makes sense to add the llapi_layout version of this interface in short order.

Also, in order to work with the kernel better, it would be useful to implement an O_TMPFILE interface for Lustre instead of using the magic filename.  The drawback is that while it should be possible to use open(O_TMPFILE | O_LOV_DELAY_CREATE) and specify the file layout, it isn't possible to specify the MDT index after the file is created as can be done with the magic filename.

commit.subject: LU-6081 user: adding llapi_create_volatile_param()
commit.message:
LU-6081 user: adding llapi_create_volatile_param()

This new function is similar to the existing
llapi_create_volatile_idx() function, but takes 2 additional
parameters: a stripe information, and a file mode.

llapi_create_volatile_idx() is changed to use
llapi_create_volatile_param().

Include a new man page for llapi_create_volatile_param(),
llapi_create_volatile_idx() and llapi_create_volatile_param().
